title: DataFolder Object
description: A data folder that contains a collection of data items.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: uploadAssembly
methods.description: Uploads a set of files that represent an assembly There should only be a single top-level assembly file but there can be any number of other files that represent sub-assemblies.

methods.name: uploadFile
methods.description: Uploads a single file to this directory.

properties.name: dataFiles
properties.description: Returns a collection containing all of the items within this folder, excluding folders. Use the dataFolders property to get the folders.

properties.name: dataFolders
properties.description: Returns a collection containing all of the folders within this folder.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Returns the unique ID for this folder. This is the same id used in the APS Data Management API.

properties.name: isRoot
properties.description: Indicates if this folder is the root folder within the parent project.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the displayed name of this folder.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentFolder
properties.description: Returns the parent folder this folder is contained within. Returns null if this is the project's root folder.

properties.name: parentProject
properties.description: Returns the parent project that owns this folder.

classType.title: DataFolder.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: DataFolder.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the deletion was successful.

uploadAssembly.title: DataFolder.uploadAssembly Method
uploadAssembly.description: Uploads a set of files that represent an assembly There should only be a single top-level assembly file but there can be any number of other files that represent sub-assemblies. Remarks
uploadAssembly.return_description: The upload process is asynchronous which means that this method will return before the upload process had completed. The returned DataFileFuture object can be used to check on the current state of the upload to determine if it is still uploading, is complete, or has failed. If it is complete the final DataFinal can be retrieved through the DataFileFuture object.

parameters.name: filenames
parameters.description: An array of strings that contains the list of all of the files that are part of the assembly. The name of the top-level assembly file must be the first file in the array.

uploadFile.title: DataFolder.uploadFile Method
uploadFile.description: Uploads a single file to this directory. Remarks
uploadFile.return_description: The upload process is asynchronous which means that this method will return before the upload process had completed. The returned DataFileFuture object can be used to check on the current state of the upload to determine if it is still uploading, is complete, or has failed. If it is complete the final DataFinal can be retrieved through the DataFileFuture object.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The full filename of the file to upload.

dataFiles.title: DataFolder.dataFiles Property
dataFiles.description: Returns a collection containing all of the items within this folder, excluding folders. Use the dataFolders property to get the folders.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dataFolder_var.dataFiles

dataFolders.description: Returns a collection containing all of the folders within this folder.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.

name.title: DataFolder.name Property
name.description: Gets and sets the displayed name of this folder.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dataFolder_var.name

# Set the value of the property.
dataFolder_var.name = propertyValue

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

parentProject.description: Returns the parent project that owns this folder.
